memory.summary: I have a free afternoon and low stress, but the term project is barely started. I should prioritize starting the project instead of taking a leisure activity.
Working 2 hours boosted progress to 0.28, confirming early effort prevents later crunch. I must maintain this pace before the afternoon slips away further.
After 2 hours, progress jumped to 0.47, confirming early work prevents crunch. I must maintain this pace before the afternoon slips away.
Two hours of focused work advanced the term project from 0.47 to 0.61, confirming that consistent early effort is effective despite my current stress and sleep debt.
Two more hours of work raised progress to 0.70, confirming the strategy prevents crunch. I must maintain this pace before the deadline in three days.
Term project progress improved to 0.78, but I missed the trivial chore deadline with zero progress. My high stress and sleep debt suggest I need to prioritize smaller tasks immediately.
Progress jumped from 0.78 to 0.86 in two hours, confirming the early start strategy works despite high stress and sleep debt.
After sleeping only 2 hours, my energy remains high at 2.4 and sleep debt is zero. This confirms that short naps effectively replenish resources without accumulating debt.
The Term project progress increased to 0.93 in just 1.5 hours, confirming that my high energy and zero sleep debt allow for rapid task completion without added stress.
The Term project is now complete, confirming that my high energy and zero sleep debt enabled rapid completion without added stress.
After 6 hours of sleep, my energy remains stable at 7.5 with zero sleep debt, confirming that adequate rest prevents resource depletion even after completing the Term project.
The Term project is fully complete. My high energy and zero sleep debt enabled rapid progress without added stress.
